name: Hoopak
special: Double weapon. Used as a melee weapon, the Hoopak is a quarterstaff: 1d6 bludgeoning (1d8 two-handed, Versatile), Topple mastery.
Used as a ranged weapon, the forked end is a sling: range 30/120, 1d4 bludgeoning, Ammunition (sling bullets or stones), Slow mastery. Switching ends costs your free Object Interaction.
Whistling fork. Swung sharply through the air the Hoopak emits a loud whoop or whistle. As a Bonus Action you can sound it to send a simple pre-arranged signal audible out to 600 feet.
Proficiency. Anyone proficient with both simple weapons and a martial weapon can wield the Hoopak fully; with simple-weapon proficiency only you can still use either end at no proficiency bonus.

modes.name: Staff
modes.summary: Quarterstaff — a versatile bludgeoning melee weapon.

modes.name: Sling
modes.summary: The forked end fires bullets or stones. Switching ends costs your free Object Interaction.

modes.name: Bullroarer
modes.summary: Swung sharply it whoops or whistles — as a Bonus Action, a simple pre-arranged signal audible out to ~600 feet.
